---
title: "Quarto 2 — Code Chunks & Tables"
subtitle: "Deeper chunks, publication-quality tables"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Goals — 60 minutes

::: {.incremental}
- Full range of chunk options
- Publication tables: `kable`, `kableExtra`, `DT`
- Clean model output with `broom`
- R + Python side-by-side via tabsets
:::

# Engines, briefly {.section-divider}

## Two, mostly invisible

| Engine | When |
|--------|------|
| `knitr` | `{r}` chunks (and mixed R+Python) |
| `jupyter` | `{python}`, `{julia}`, `{ojs}` alone |

::: {.fragment}
Override only if needed: `engine: knitr` or `engine: jupyter` in YAML.
:::

# Tables {.section-divider}

## Why we don't just print

```{.r}
head(biomarker_data, 5)
```

::: {.fragment}
Functional. Ugly. Not for reports.
:::

## kable — the baseline

```{.r}
biomarker_data |>
  group_by(site) |>
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    mean_pfoa = mean(pfoa_serum)
  ) |>
  kable(caption = "Mean PFOA by site")
```

Works in HTML, Word, PDF. Simple, reliable.

## kableExtra — publication quality

```{.r}
... |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
    full_width = FALSE
  ) |>
  row_spec(elevated_rows,
           bold = TRUE, color = "#DC2626") |>
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "PFAS" = 2, "Other" = 2)) |>
  footnote(general = "...")
```

::: {.fragment}
Conditional row formatting, grouped headers, footnotes — all available.
:::

## DT — interactive HTML

```{.r}
DT::datatable(participants,
              filter = "top",
              options = list(pageLength = 10))
```

::: {.incremental}
- Searchable, sortable, paginated
- **HTML only** — falls back in Word/PDF
:::

## broom — clean model output

```{.r}
model <- lm(log(pfoa_serum) ~ age + bmi + sex, data = biomarker_data)

tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling()
```

::: {.fragment}
`tidy()` for coefficients. `glance()` for fit statistics. Both data frames — pipe directly into `kable()`.
:::

## When to use which

| Method | Best for | Formats |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `kable()` | Simple, reliable | HTML/Word/PDF |
| `kableExtra` | Publication quality | HTML (best) |
| `DT` | Interactive | HTML only |
| `gt` | Highly customized | HTML/PDF |

# Quarto-distinctive chunk options {.section-divider}

## Five that earn their keep

::: {.incremental}
- `cache: true` — skip slow code when unchanged
- `code-fold: true` — collapse code behind a click
- `output: asis` — let output write markdown
- `dev: svg` — vector figures
- `echo: fenced` — show the chunk header (teaching docs)
:::

# Mixed R + Python {.section-divider}

## Tabsets

````markdown
::: {.panel-tabset}

### R
```{r}
ggplot(biomarker_data, aes(pfoa_serum, cholesterol)) +
  geom_point()
```

### Python
```{python}
sns.scatterplot(data=r.biomarker_data, x="pfoa_serum", y="cholesterol")
```

:::
````

::: {.fragment}
Tabs in HTML; sequential in Word/PDF. Excellent for mixed-language teams.
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Notebook Quarto 2

::: {.callout-tip}
Open `afternoon_quarto/02_code_chunks_and_tables.qmd`. Exercises 2.1, 2.2, 2.3.
:::

Back in 60 min — then figures and visualizations.
